summary: Full backend and frontend testing of Vrax Voyage premium flight comparator site. Fixed critical bug in add comment API (MongoDB ObjectId serialization error). All backend APIs pass. All frontend pages render correctly with luxury dark theme.

critical.issue: Returned 500 Internal Server Error due to MongoDB ObjectId not being JSON serializable after insert_one(). FIXED: added doc.pop('_id', None) before returning response.

issues: Like button click failed with 'Element is not visible' - could not test like button on article page directly via playwright, but like counts visible on blog listing page

action_items: Verify like button on article page /blog/maldives-paradis-ocean is accessible (not hidden behind overlay)
critical_code_review_comments: MongoDB ObjectId _id must always be excluded from all insert_one returns - check all other DB write operations in server.py for same issue (contact endpoint was also susceptible but happened to work because it returned a static message)

success_rate.frontend: 95% (all pages load, affiliate links correct, contact form works, legal pages correct)

seed_data_creation: None - seed data already exists in DB from server startup
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend bug fixed: add_comment endpoint was returning 500 due to MongoDB _id ObjectId serialization. Fixed by adding doc.pop('_id', None). All 9 backend tests pass. All frontend routes verified: /, /blog, /blog/maldives-paradis-ocean, /contact, /mentions-legales, /cgu, /confidentialite, /affiliation. Affiliate links confirmed: TravelPayouts marker=704469, DiscoverCars a_aid=704469, KiwiTaxi marker=704469, HotelLook marker=704469, Amazon tag=vrax-21. Contact form submits successfully. Atmani Bachir appears in contact and legal pages. Blog shows 6 articles with tag filters.
rca_of_issue: Root cause: POST /api/blog/posts/{post_id}/comments - after calling db.comments.insert_one(doc), MongoDB modifies the doc dict in-place by adding _id (ObjectId). When the function returned {**doc, ...}, FastAPI tried to serialize ObjectId which is not JSON serializable, causing 500. Fix: doc.pop('_id', None) before return.
